findings: Verified Indian Peafowl (Pavo cristatus), Phasianidae. The adult male’s cobalt-blue head, neck and breast; fan-tipped crest; white supraorbital stripe and lower white facial patch; bronze-green train with eyespots; barred/scalloped wing coverts; pale gray-brown legs and visible spur agree with the supplied field photos and verified descriptions.

All visible plate text is substantively correct: common/scientific name, family, measurements (0.86–2.12 m; 1.4–1.6 m wingspan; 2.7–6 kg), ground-foraging/diurnal behavior, escape into cover, tree roosting, habitat, and field marks. The plate contains no place name, ZIP code, coordinates, map, or local-observation detail.

The illustrated bird has exactly one head, one beak, two wings, two legs, and one tail/train. Minor non-material issue: the right-side 0–2 m ruler reads as standing height, whereas the stated maximum length includes the long train and can exceed that vertical measure.
